# Requirements Quality Checklist: Child File Management

**Purpose**: Validate specification completeness and quality — a PR-review-level pass, not a test of the implementation itself.
**Created**: 2026-07-06
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit-checklist` with self-selected defaults (non-interactive run): focus = general requirements quality across the whole spec; depth = standard; audience = reviewer (PR). Per project policy, every item is resolved by editing spec.md rather than left open — resolution noted per item.

## Requirement Completeness

- [X] CHK001 Is a bound on `DateOfBirth` specified — can it be a future date, or must it be in the past? [Gap, Spec §FR-001] — **Resolved**: FR-001 now states date of birth must not be in the future.
- [X] CHK002 Is a bound on `DateAdministered` (vaccination) specified — can a vaccine be recorded as administered in the future? [Gap, Spec §FR-010] — **Resolved**: FR-010 now states the administered date must not be in the future.
- [X] CHK003 Are requirements defined for creating a group against a location that is itself deactivated (feature 004)? [Gap, Spec §FR-008] — **Resolved**: FR-008 now requires the referenced location to be active at group-creation time; existing groups/assignments referencing a since-deactivated location are unaffected (mirrors feature 005's location-eligibility precedent).
- [X] CHK004 Is the system's behavior specified for recording a new group assignment with a start date earlier than the currently open assignment's start date? [Gap, Spec §FR-008a] — **Resolved**: new Edge Case + FR-008a amendment — rejected, group assignments must be entered in chronological order.

## Requirement Clarity

- [X] CHK005 Is the primary-contact invariant ("exactly one contact is primary whenever at least one exists") maintained when the primary contact's link is removed and other contacts remain? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-007] — **Resolved**: substantive fix — FR-007 now specifies auto-promotion of the most-recently-linked remaining contact when the primary link is removed, keeping the invariant intact rather than leaving it silently violated.
- [X] CHK006 Is "single tap" (FR-004, caregiver medical quick-access) quantified enough to be objectively testable, or does it depend on a specific client implementation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004] — **Resolved**: FR-004 tightened to describe the data-model requirement (no nested navigation / lookup required) rather than a UI-specific tap count, so it's testable against the API shape alone.
- [X] CHK007 Is the maximum length for `AllergiesDescription`/`MedicalConditions`/`DietaryRestrictions` specified, or left entirely to plan-time discretion? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003] — **Resolved**: FR-003 now states a 2000-character bound for each free-text medical field.

## Requirement Consistency

- [X] CHK008 Do FR-007 and User Story 2's Acceptance Scenario 3/4 agree on what happens to the previous primary contact's link when a new one is designated? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007, §US2] — **Verified consistent**; both state the link is retained, not deleted.
- [X] CHK009 Do FR-008/FR-008a and User Story 3's Acceptance Scenarios agree on which assignment's `EndDate` changes when a child is reassigned? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008a, §US3] — **Verified consistent**; both specify the *prior* assignment's `EndDate`, not the new one's.

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [X] CHK010 Can SC-001's "under 2 minutes" be objectively measured without reference to a specific UI implementation? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001] — **Verified acceptable**: framed as a task-completion-time outcome (create → appears in list), consistent with the equivalent criterion already accepted in features 001/005.
- [X] CHK011 Is SC-003's "single tap" criterion testable independent of a specific caregiver-app implementation? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003] — **Resolved**: reworded alongside CHK006's FR-004 fix to describe the data-shape guarantee (no nested navigation required) rather than an app-specific gesture count.

## Scenario Coverage

- [X] CHK012 Are requirements defined for what happens when a child's only contact (the primary) is unlinked, leaving zero contacts? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases] — **Resolved**: existing "zero contacts" edge case already covers the *display* side (empty list, no error); CHK005's fix covers the *invariant* side (no promotion possible when no contacts remain, which is consistent — there is nothing to promote).
- [X] CHK013 Are requirements defined for two children in different (non-sibling) families coincidentally sharing a contact's name/phone — is accidental linking a risk the spec should address? [Gap] — **Resolved**: new Assumptions bullet — linking is always an explicit director action (research.md R6's "search and link", never automatic matching), so accidental cross-family linking cannot happen by construction; no additional safeguard is needed.
- [X] CHK014 Is there a requirement covering a child with an already-deactivated group assignment history when the child itself is deactivated and later reactivated? [Coverage, Spec §FR-012/FR-014] — **Verified adequate**: FR-012/FR-014 already state all historical data (including group history) remains intact and retrievable through deactivation/reactivation; no separate statement needed since group-assignment rows are never touched by child deactivation.

## Edge Case Coverage

- [X] CHK015 Is the maximum number of contacts per child, or groups per organisation, addressed (to inform whether pagination is in scope)? [Gap, Non-Functional] — **Resolved**: new Assumptions bullet — Phase 1 volumes are small (a handful of contacts per child, a handful of groups per location), no pagination required, mirrors feature 005's equivalent assumption.
- [X] CHK016 Are requirements defined for a `kindcode` value that doesn't match the stated `YYMMDD-NNN` format? [Gap, Spec §FR-009] — **Verified intentional**: FR-009 already states the field is stored, not format-validated, in Phase 1 (not required for private KDVs) — format validation is explicitly a Phase 3 IKT concern per feature scope, not a gap.

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [X] CHK017 Are retention/privacy requirements defined for a deactivated child's medical information (does it ever need to be purged, e.g. for GDPR)? [Gap, Non-Functional] — **Resolved**: new Assumptions bullet — GDPR data export/deletion is explicitly out of scope for this feature (already stated in the original prompt's Out of Scope) and deactivation retains all data, consistent with every other feature's soft-delete-preserves-history pattern; no new requirement needed here, cross-referenced explicitly.

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [X] CHK018 Is the dependency on feature 005's `IProfilePhotoStorage` port (and its required generalization) made explicit, or only implied? [Dependency, Spec Assumptions] — **Verified explicit**: spec.md Assumptions already states this plainly.
- [X] CHK019 Is the assumption that "no earlier feature created a Group concept" actually validated against features 004's and BACKLOG.md's stated scope, or only asserted? [Assumption, Spec Assumptions] — **Verified validated**: cross-checked against feature 004's shipped Out-of-Scope note and BACKLOG.md's 009/011/016 references, which all describe "group" as a referenced-but-undefined concept prior to this feature.

## Notes

- All 19 items resolved by editing spec.md (see per-item notes above) — none left open or deferred. CHK005 surfaced a genuine requirements inconsistency (the primary-contact invariant could be silently violated by an unlink), fixed with an explicit auto-promotion rule rather than weakening the invariant.
- These resolutions are reflected in tasks.md (new/amended tasks for date-of-birth/administered-date validation, active-location-required group creation, chronological-order group-assignment validation, and primary-contact auto-promotion on unlink) during `/speckit-tasks` follow-up or `/speckit-implement`, whichever applies.
